items.text: While we directly import only a small portion of our merchandise, more than half of the goods we sell originate from China.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new fact about import exposure to China not previously disclosed in this form.

items.text: While our business is exclusively in brick-and-mortar stores, consumer e-commerce spending continues to increase.
items.rationale: Highlights a specific new risk of exclusive reliance on brick-and-mortar stores amid rising e-commerce, a material competitive threat.

items.text: Advancements in technology (including artificial intelligence or other emerging technologies) will present opportunities to inform merchandising, pricing, assortment, and other key business decisions; however, there are costs, risks and potential adverse consequences from premature adoption or over-reliance on those emerging technologies.
items.rationale: Introduces a new specific risk related to AI/emerging technology adoption and potential adverse consequences.

items.text: At the same time, if competitors successfully implement these capabilities more quickly or effectively than we do, our competitive position could be adversely affected.
items.rationale: Builds on the technology risk theme by adding competitive implications, expanding rather than introducing a wholly new risk.

items.text: Further, expanding into new markets or increasing store growth in regions where we have limited operating experience could potentially result in operational inefficiencies and increased costs.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk about operational inefficiencies from expansion into unfamiliar markets.

items.text: More than half of the merchandise we sell is originally manufactured in China.
items.rationale: Restates the same China sourcing fact as sentence 0 in different wording.
